LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT BRUCE SOLAR'S MOTION TO COMPEL ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF SATISFACTION OF JUDGMENT AND FOR AN ORDER THAT THE COURT CLERK ENTER SATISFACTION OF JUDGMENT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED ESTABLISHES THAT SOME PORTION OF THE JUDGMENT WAS NOT PAID, AND, IN ANY EVENT, DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO CARRY HIS BURDEN OF PROVING THAT THE ENTIRE JUDGMENT HAS BEEN SATISFIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR #7489

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Oct-18-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Geoffrey Faust, Esq., 925-673-1988 for plaintifff and moving party Amy Faust

Nicholas Carlin, Esq. of Phillips, Erlewine & Given, 415-398-0900 for defendant and opposingparty Bruce Solar

RULING:
A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling ON PLAINTIFF AMY K. FAUST'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs. Fees awarded per CCP 724.80 IN THE AMOUNT OF $5,250.00. Prevailing party to submit form of order.Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189. (301/PJB/CK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Sep-29-2006 Continued To Oct-18-06 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. Plaintiff Amy K. Faust's Motion for Attorney's Fees and Costs is continued to October 18, 2006 on the Court's own motion. (301/PJB) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Acknowledgment Of Satisfaction Of Judgment And For An Order That The Court Clerk Enter Satisfaction Of Judgment; Declarations Of Bruce Solar, David Strunk, Carol Davis, And Nicholas A. Carlin In Support
Off Calendar Aug-04-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT Bruce Solar's Motion To Compel Acknowledgment Of Satisfaction Of Judgment And For An Order That The Court Clerk Enter Satisfaction Of Judgment is denied. The evidence presented establishes that some portion of the judgment was not paid, and, in any event, defendant has failed to carry his burden of proving that the entire judgment has been satisfied. Prevailing party to prepare form of order. (301/PJB/DM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR 7489
